title: BRIPPED Scan for Evaluation of Emergency Department (ED) Patients With Shortness of Breath
text:
Summary: The B-RIPPED scan is a standardized ultrasound evaluation of pulmonary B-lines, Right ventricle size and strain, Inferior Vena Cava collapsibility, Pleural and Pericardial Effusion, Pneumothorax, Ejection Fraction, and lower extremity Deep Venous Thrombosis. Primary outcomes measured are the magnitude of change in differential diagnoses.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 Subjects are included if they are between the ages of 18-89 and present to the Emergency Department with a chief complaint of shortness of breath or dyspnea. 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 known history of asthma, 

 are 20 or more weeks pregnant, or 

 have had thoraco-abdominal trauma in the past 72 hours.
